rag_rules: Generate annex agree blocks only for component type or implementation scope as dictated by the owning component declaration.
Use assume/guarantee in component type annexes for input envelopes and output dependencies using only visible features.
Use assign only in implementation annexes for output features or declared auxiliary variables, never in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
generic_rules: Restrict AGREE expressions to identifiers explicitly visible in the current component scope.
Base all contract clauses on concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations from the model, not abstract or inferred values.
Maintain scope integrity: component type contracts must not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use annex agree {** ... **}; for all AGREE declarations.
Declare constants with const name : type = value; and equations with eq name : type = expression;
anti_patterns: Avoid placing guarantee clauses in implementation annexes.
Do not invent or copy identifiers from examples—use only those present in the current AADL model index.
Never encode AADL property names (e.g., Stack_Size, Period) as AGREE variables without explicit exposure.
irrelevant_topics: assume/guarantee placement strategy for specific requirements
